jokes.text: I'm in connecticut, i haven't eaten, and i'm stressed about an "away toilet" situation.
jokes.setup: Being told to loosen up at a party
jokes.punchline: Liz lists three very specific and neurotic concerns about being away from home
jokes.explanation: Perfect Liz Lemon character comedy - the specific phrase 'away toilet situation' is brilliantly neurotic and could only come from her

jokes.text: Is this potpourri or chips? 'Cause i'm gonna try to eat it.
jokes.setup: At an upscale party with rich people
jokes.punchline: Liz admits she'll eat potpourri if it might be food
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Liz's food obsession and social awkwardness at fancy events

jokes.text: Of course,that was back when Jet was actually about jet ownership. That magazine took a weird turn.
jokes.setup: Explaining Gavin was on Fortune and Jet magazine covers
jokes.punchline: Casual reference to Jet magazine changing from private jets to something else entirely
jokes.explanation: Clever reference to real Jet magazine (African-American publication) vs assumed private jet magazine

jokes.text: These are not chips.
jokes.setup: Earlier wondered if potpourri was chips
jokes.punchline: Deadpan confirmation after apparently trying to eat potpourri
jokes.explanation: Perfect callback structure - the understated delivery after presumably eating potpourri is perfectly timed

jokes.text: Because of my severe agoraphobia and my debilitating wealth...
jokes.setup: Explaining why he hosts dinner parties
jokes.punchline: Treating extreme wealth as a medical condition parallel to agoraphobia
jokes.explanation: 'Debilitating wealth' is a brilliant oxymoron that perfectly captures absurd rich person problems

jokes.text: The world of fashion, society, art collecting and yelling...
jokes.setup: Listing sophisticated walks of life at his dinner
jokes.punchline: Ending the elegant list with 'yelling' as if it's a refined pursuit
jokes.explanation: Perfect list escalation joke - builds expectation of refinement then crashes it absurdly

jokes.text: Why isn't there any good art in here? Come on!
jokes.setup: Gavin just mentioned art collecting as one of his guest categories
jokes.punchline: Guest immediately proves the point by loudly criticizing the art
jokes.explanation: Good immediate callback that validates the 'yelling' category while deflating Gavin's sophistication

jokes.text: Funny story,i was only wearing that because the fire alarm went off while i was getting a haircut.
jokes.setup: Being complimented on a photo in the NY Times style section
jokes.punchline: Her one moment of looking good was purely accidental due to emergency
jokes.explanation: Very Liz - unable to accept a compliment and revealing the unglamorous truth behind her one stylish moment

jokes.text: I keep one chair empty, to represent our unfulfilled hopes and dreams. I'm just kidding, eliot spitzer's hooker couldn't make it.
jokes.setup: Pretentious explanation for empty chair
jokes.punchline: Reveals it's actually for a scandal-associated sex worker
jokes.explanation: Brilliant misdirection - sets up pretentious art-speak then crashes into tabloid reality. Perfect structure and timing.

jokes.text: McEnroe, give me a break. I'm on it. 77th and amsterdam, 68th and columbus, 125th and president clinton boulevard
jokes.setup: Someone suggesting a cupcake place
jokes.punchline: Jack instantly knows multiple NYC cupcake locations with street precision
jokes.explanation: Perfect Jack character moment - his unexpected expertise and competitive nature about knowing NYC food

jokes.text: Also,don't over think it, sara lee,frozen, unbelievable.
jokes.setup: Just rattled off sophisticated cupcake locations
jokes.punchline: Casual recommendation for frozen grocery store cake
jokes.explanation: Good deflation after the fancy cupcake knowledge - Jack's surprising pragmatism

jokes.text: You know my two sons, tracy,jr. And george foreman?
jokes.setup: Introducing his sons
jokes.punchline: One son is named after the famous boxer/grill salesman
jokes.explanation: Perfectly absurd Tracy move to name his kid after George Foreman - fits his celebrity obsession

jokes.text: This is voodoo,ken! Those two are up to something.
jokes.setup: Kids made him something cute
jokes.punchline: Tracy interprets children's art as supernatural threat
jokes.explanation: Classic Tracy paranoia - taking innocent kid behavior and making it sinister conspiracy

jokes.text: I know i was a handful when my family moved from our farm house to that militia camp in the woods.
jokes.setup: Offering advice about kids acting out during big changes
jokes.punchline: Casually reveals deeply disturbing childhood backstory
jokes.explanation: Perfect Kenneth joke - delivers horrifying personal history with cheerful innocence. The contrast is expertly crafted.

jokes.text: the life-sized tracy jordan sex doll. They're selling like hotcakes in japan.
jokes.setup: Discussing new income streams
jokes.punchline: Matter-of-fact mention of his own sex doll being popular overseas
jokes.explanation: Absurd but delivered matter-of-factly - sets up payoff later. Tracy's casual acceptance of bizarre merchandising

jokes.text: I thought that was just a tagline for my movie,death bank.
jokes.setup: Kenneth saying 'money is the root of all evil'
jokes.punchline: Tracy thinks the famous saying was invented for his bad movie
jokes.explanation: Perfect Tracy ego/ignorance combo - doesn't know common sayings but thinks everything relates to his work

jokes.text: It's like picasso not painting, or bruce willis not combing action and rock harmonica.
jokes.setup: Comparing Jack being out of business to famous people not doing their thing
jokes.punchline: Second comparison is to Bruce Willis's nonexistent musical career
jokes.explanation: Brilliant escalation - starts with obvious Picasso reference then veers into absurd Bruce Willis musical career

jokes.text: Here's the pitch, wind power, bandwidth,chinese market. Oh,god.
jokes.setup: Gavin pitching buzzword business ideas
jokes.punchline: Jack's immediate orgasmic reaction to corporate buzzwords
jokes.explanation: Perfect Jack character moment - his sexual arousal to meaningless business buzzwords

jokes.text: And i was pulled onstage once to dance at a bruce springsteen concert.
jokes.setup: Saying this is the most exciting day of his life
jokes.punchline: His previous most exciting day was awkwardly dancing at a concert
jokes.explanation: Good character detail showing Jack's surprising fanboy moment and social awkwardness

jokes.text: you can run for political office Without having to pretend to be a fundamentalist.
jokes.setup: Talking about being rich enough for politics
jokes.punchline: Cynical observation about fake religious pandering in politics
jokes.explanation: Sharp political satire about wealth buying political authenticity

jokes.text: I wonder if carl here might say good night to you for me? Carl? Good night,liz. I hope to see you again soon.
jokes.setup: Gavin can't touch people due to germ phobia
jokes.punchline: Has his security guard deliver romantic farewell for him
jokes.explanation: Brilliant visual/conceptual gag - romantic proxy through intimidating security guard. Sets up later revelation

jokes.text: Terrorist attacks, cloverfieldmonsters. I can't do it,because my head is too big for the helmet.
jokes.setup: Listing emergency scenarios needing a marshal
jokes.punchline: Adding movie monsters to real threats, then Pete's prosaic excuse
jokes.explanation: Good escalation from real to absurd threats, plus Pete's self-deprecating physical excuse

jokes.text: It is. Wow,you have,like, dog ears.
jokes.setup: Liz asking if her phone is ringing
jokes.punchline: Cerie's casual, insulting observation about Liz's hearing
jokes.explanation: Perfect Cerie - obliviously insulting while being helpful. Good cringe moment for Liz

jokes.text: Listen,i only took that napkin because i wrapped some chicken in it.
jokes.setup: Gavin calling Liz
jokes.punchline: Liz immediately confesses to minor theft, assuming that's why he's calling
jokes.explanation: Perfect Liz neurosis - immediately confessing to imagined wrongdoing, revealing her food hoarding

jokes.text: Well... This must be what the kids call "a booty call." I haven't been out of the house since 2004, and even i know the kids don't call it that anymore.
jokes.setup: Liz trying to be hip about modern dating
jokes.punchline: Even the recluse knows she's using outdated slang
jokes.explanation: Good generational humor - Liz's attempt at being current backfires even compared to a hermit

jokes.text: I'll send a helicopter to take you to the train station.
jokes.setup: Making plans for a date
jokes.punchline: Extremely expensive transport to reach normal transport
jokes.explanation: Perfect rich person absurdity - unnecessary luxury for mundane purpose

jokes.text: What the what?
jokes.setup: Bizarre phone conversation ending
jokes.punchline: Liz's signature confused reaction
jokes.explanation: Classic Liz catchphrase - not original but perfectly deployed and highly quotable

jokes.text: My kids are going to kill me!
jokes.setup: Watching TV about Mendez brothers murdering parents
jokes.punchline: Tracy connects it directly to his paranoid fears
jokes.explanation: Good callback to his earlier paranoia - the TV show triggers his specific fears

jokes.text: After years of inbreeding, the pigs are getting violent. And the pig shield around the house has worn thin.
jokes.setup: Explaining why he needs to send money home
jokes.punchline: Casually mentioning inbred violent pigs and pig-based home defense
jokes.explanation: Exceptional Kenneth joke - 'pig shield' is brilliant absurdist worldbuilding delivered with innocent sincerity. Among the episode's best.

jokes.text: Well,that would be great if pigs weren't excellent swimmers.
jokes.setup: Jack suggesting he buy a pig moat
jokes.punchline: Kenneth matter-of-factly rejecting the plan due to pig swimming ability
jokes.explanation: Perfect deadpan delivery of absurd pig knowledge - the casual expertise is hilarious

jokes.text: He asked if we were involved, i said you used to have a thing for me, but you were getting over it. That is what's happening,right?
jokes.setup: Explaining conversation with Gavin about Liz
jokes.punchline: Jack's oblivious assumption about Liz's feelings for him
jokes.explanation: Good Jack arrogance moment - his casual assumption about being irresistible to Liz

jokes.text: I just wish i could start a relationship about 12 years in, When you really don't have to try anymore and you can just sit around together and goof on tv shows, And then go to bed without anybody trying any funny business.
jokes.setup: Jack asking what she wants in relationships
jokes.punchline: Liz wants to skip to the boring, sexless part of marriage
jokes.explanation: Brilliant Liz character moment - her romantic goals are anti-romantic but relatable to many viewers

jokes.text: If i were dating a man, he would be the top of my list with michael jordan, denzel washington,taye diggs... God,do i have a black thing?
jokes.setup: Praising Gavin as dateworthy
jokes.punchline: Jack's dawning realization about his attraction patterns
jokes.explanation: Good uncomfortable realization moment - Jack's oblivious self-discovery about racial fetishization

jokes.text: you're going to work this thing like a chinese gymnast. Wear something tight, force a smile, and lie about your age.
jokes.setup: Giving Liz dating advice
jokes.punchline: Extremely problematic comparison to exploited child athletes
jokes.explanation: Dark humor about athletic exploitation - fits Jack's corporate callousness but walks ethical line

jokes.text: Don't menendez me.
jokes.setup: His kids greeting him enthusiastically
jokes.punchline: Tracy turns the murder case into a verb for his situation
jokes.explanation: Brilliant callback with wordplay - turning the murder case into a verb is creative and perfectly Tracy

jokes.text: one of these benches is a priceless work of art, And the other is an ordinary bench from which to admire it. Which one's which?
jokes.setup: Showing off his art collection
jokes.punchline: Pretentious art game with identical-looking benches
jokes.explanation: Good satire of pretentious modern art - the indistinguishable benches mock art world absurdity

jokes.text: I'd be lying if i told you i never danced with a broom.
jokes.setup: Admitting isolation gets lonely
jokes.punchline: Specific, pathetic detail about dancing with cleaning supplies
jokes.explanation: Good loneliness humor - specific enough to be pathetic without being too sad

jokes.text: sometimes, to feel like i have company during dinner, I dispute credit card charges on speaker phone.
jokes.setup: Sharing her own loneliness coping mechanisms
jokes.punchline: Using customer service calls as dinner conversation
jokes.explanation: Excellent Liz moment - the specificity and relatability of urban loneliness coping mechanisms

jokes.text: We can never leave the house, and we'd just probably sit around all weekend and watch tv. Go on.
jokes.setup: Gavin describing the downsides of his situation
jokes.punchline: Liz finds his 'problems' increasingly appealing
jokes.explanation: Perfect callback to Liz's earlier relationship goals - what he sees as problems are her dreams

jokes.text: we could never be physically intimate. I know. What woman would want all that?
jokes.setup: Gavin listing another 'downside' of dating him
jokes.punchline: Liz's enthusiasm for sexless relationship
jokes.explanation: Excellent payoff to earlier setup - Liz's 'I know' reveals perfect compatibility for wrong reasons

jokes.text: I did some street performing in my neighborhood this weekend.
jokes.setup: Kenneth has another $10 to invest
jokes.punchline: Implies he was busking for extra money
jokes.explanation: Good character moment - imagining Kenneth doing street performance for small change

jokes.text: I'm just kidding. The middle class is dying, you'll be renting forever.
jokes.setup: Encouraging Kenneth about home ownership
jokes.punchline: Immediately crushing his dreams with economic reality
jokes.explanation: Perfect Jack cruelty - builds hope then destroys it with casual economic truth

jokes.text: you'd have been better off putting that money in a coffee can.
jokes.setup: Explaining how badly the investment went
jokes.punchline: Old-fashioned cash hiding would have been safer
jokes.explanation: Solid financial humor - the old-school reference emphasizes how badly modern investing went

jokes.text: Yeah. They can't seem to get anything right up there, can they?
jokes.setup: Watching MTV Canada together
jokes.punchline: Casual nationalism about Canadian TV inadequacy
jokes.explanation: Mild observational humor about Canadian media - not particularly strong but character-appropriate

jokes.text: I'm not really an eccentric agoraphobe, I just tell people that so they won't know i'm under house arrest for tax fraud. And arson.
jokes.setup: Revealing the truth about his situation
jokes.punchline: Adding arson casually to the already serious tax fraud
jokes.explanation: Good escalation - tax fraud is serious, but adding arson as an afterthought is perfect

jokes.text: I miscounted the men,liz! I miscounted the men!
jokes.setup: Dramatic escape sequence
jokes.punchline: Gavin's panicked confession about surveillance counting error
jokes.explanation: Good action-comedy moment - the specific error that caused the crisis is mundane but urgent

jokes.text: What is racketeering? No one knows,lemon.
jokes.setup: Liz asking about the charges against Gavin
jokes.punchline: Jack's matter-of-fact assertion that the crime is unknowable
jokes.explanation: Excellent observational humor - racketeering IS confusingly defined, and Jack's certainty about the uncertainty is perfect

jokes.text: I thought i was helping him when i let him hold my boob while we watched top chef.
jokes.setup: Explaining she thought he was a germaphobe
jokes.punchline: Reveals awkward intimate moment she allowed for his 'condition'
jokes.explanation: Good cringe moment - the specific detail and Liz's misguided charity make it both uncomfortable and funny

jokes.text: I'm going to hit gavin volure harder than a bottle of whiskey in an irish wake.
jokes.setup: Jack promising to fix the situation
jokes.punchline: Oddly specific ethnic stereotype comparison
jokes.explanation: Decent Jack bravado moment but relies on ethnic stereotype - not his strongest material

jokes.text: That's not me. That's the tracy jordan japanese sex doll. You can tell us apart because it's not suffering from a vitamin deficiency.
jokes.setup: Confusion about Tracy's presence
jokes.punchline: Tracy points out the doll is healthier than him
jokes.explanation: Good callback with self-deprecating twist - Tracy comparing unfavorably to his own sex doll

jokes.text: To use as a decoy. So my greedy children will murder it, and i'll be able to escape un-mendezed.
jokes.setup: Asked why he got the sex doll
jokes.punchline: Elaborate plan using sex doll as murder decoy
jokes.explanation: Brilliant callback combining multiple elements - the verb 'un-mendezed' and absurd decoy plan are perfectly Tracy

jokes.text: Both families are rich, both families have two sons, and both families are staples of court tv.
jokes.setup: Comparing his family to the Mendez family
jokes.punchline: Adding that both families are Court TV regulars
jokes.explanation: Good escalation building the parallel - the Court TV detail is a nice self-aware Tracy touch

jokes.text: INNOVATION TOMORROW AMERICA SUNSTREAM You can't be broke, what about this house?
jokes.setup: Jack questioning how Sunstream could be fake
jokes.punchline: Gavin reveals their commercials were meaningless buzzwords
jokes.explanation: Brilliant satire of corporate advertising - the meaningless buzzword montage is perfectly executed

jokes.text: Hey,glenn, that's my peanut butter. Creamy? This is-- this is not my peanut-- i'm sorry,glenn.
jokes.setup: Gavin explaining he's broke and pathetic
jokes.punchline: Interrupted by petty roommate conflict about peanut butter
jokes.explanation: Good deflation moment - shows how far he's fallen through mundane white-collar prison dynamics

jokes.text: Damn it. I have got a serious case of the mondays.
jokes.setup: Gavin expressing frustration with his situation
jokes.punchline: Using office cliché for serious criminal/romantic crisis
jokes.explanation: Decent understatement humor - trivial phrase for major life crisis shows his disconnect

jokes.text: God,these acoustics in here are fantastic. Tomorrow,tomorrow i love you tomorrow it's only a day
jokes.setup: Hiding in what's presumably a bathroom during confrontation
jokes.punchline: Gets distracted by good acoustics and starts singing Annie
jokes.explanation: Good absurdist moment - Gavin's priorities and musical theater obsession emerge at wrong time

jokes.text: Well,what would this country be if our economy didn't allow wealthy people to take advantage of rubes?
jokes.setup: Jack apologizing for losing Kenneth's money
jokes.punchline: Reframing exploitation as American economic principle
jokes.explanation: Excellent Jack moment - turning personal guilt into systemic justification with perfect corporate logic

jokes.text: We parcells have eaten our share of rock soup and squirrel tail. But we've also known lean times.
jokes.setup: Explaining his family will survive financial hardship
jokes.punchline: Implies rock soup and squirrel tail were the GOOD times
jokes.explanation: Exceptional Kenneth joke - the misdirection about what constitutes hardship is perfectly structured and character-specific

jokes.text: Doesn't that responsibility come with a $4,000 signing bonus? Uh... Yes?
jokes.setup: Jack asking about emergency marshal job
jokes.punchline: Pete realizes he's being manipulated but plays along
jokes.explanation: Good setup for helping Kenneth - Pete's reluctant cooperation shows the office dynamics

jokes.text: I dreamed that you would get so rich that you would leave us and get a new family. And never come back. And daddy... I don't want you to leave us. 'Cause i love you.
jokes.setup: Tracy's paranoid expectations about his kids
jokes.punchline: Kid reveals innocent, loving motivation for attention
jokes.explanation: Good reversal of Tracy's paranoia - the truth is heartwarming instead of murderous

jokes.text: Stop it. Stop patriciding!
jokes.setup: His son expressing love and affection
jokes.punchline: Tracy turns 'patricide' into a verb for the loving behavior
jokes.explanation: Brilliant wordplay - using 'patriciding' as a verb for loving behavior shows Tracy's continued paranoid misinterpretation

jokes.text: If anything ever happens to me, you and your brother are going to go to jail.
jokes.setup: Tracy finally understanding his kids love him
jokes.punchline: Threatens them with consequences for any future murder
jokes.explanation: Good dark resolution - Tracy accepts their love but maintains some paranoia as insurance

jokes.text: Also everything worked out with jenna's dad visiting. What? Oh,you weren't really around for any of that.
jokes.setup: Tracy mentioning resolution to subplot
jokes.punchline: Meta acknowledgment that Liz missed a whole storyline
jokes.explanation: Good meta-humor about TV storytelling - acknowledging the A/B plot structure explicitly

jokes.text: Cheap cholesterol medicine from canada. A paris hilton film released only in canada. Camp of approval? What does that even mean?
jokes.setup: Gavin proving he was in Canada
jokes.punchline: Bizarre Canadian-only Paris Hilton movie with nonsensical title
jokes.explanation: Good absurdist details about Canadian cultural differences - the movie title is particularly bizarre

jokes.text: Toronto was just like new york, but without all the stuff.
jokes.setup: Describing his experience in Canada
jokes.punchline: Reducing Toronto to 'New York minus stuff'
jokes.explanation: Nice economical putdown of Toronto - the vague 'stuff' makes it funnier than specific criticisms

jokes.text: taking advantage of her loneliness and her big ben-sized biological clock.
jokes.setup: Jack defending Liz from Gavin
jokes.punchline: Describing Liz's biological clock as monumentally sized
jokes.explanation: Good metaphor for Liz's dating desperation - Big Ben comparison is vivid and specific

jokes.text: I can't believe i'm saying this to the great gavin volure, but liz lemon is too good for you.
jokes.setup: Jack's usual attitude toward Liz
jokes.punchline: Rare moment of Jack defending Liz's worth
jokes.explanation: Nice character moment - Jack's protective instinct overrides his usual condescension toward Liz

jokes.text: I swear i will paint you. I am not going to real prison.
jokes.setup: Gavin threatening people with a paint brush
jokes.punchline: Treating paint brush as dangerous weapon, then clarifying prison type
jokes.explanation: Good absurdist threat - paint brush as weapon plus distinction between white-collar and 'real' prison

jokes.text: That paint is drying weird, and now prison?
jokes.setup: Gavin listing his problems before jumping
jokes.punchline: Putting paint texture issues on same level as imprisonment
jokes.explanation: Perfect character priorities - artistic concerns rated equally with legal consequences

jokes.text: Good god,volure, that's got to be 15, 16 feet!
jokes.setup: Gavin threatening to jump from balcony
jokes.punchline: Jack's underwhelmed assessment of the jump height
jokes.explanation: Good deflation of suicide drama - Jack's practical assessment undermines the theatrical gesture

jokes.text: Does anyone need water or saltines? I would feel safer with some saltines.
jokes.setup: Kenneth taking charge as emergency marshal
jokes.punchline: His emergency response focuses on mild comfort snacks
jokes.explanation: Good Kenneth moment - his emergency leadership style involves snack distribution

jokes.text: you'll probably be killed in the first six months of prison. but that's neither here nor there,
jokes.setup: Jack trying to talk Gavin down from jumping
jokes.punchline: Casually mentions likely prison murder then dismisses it
jokes.explanation: Excellent dark humor - terrible advice delivered with casual dismissal, perfect Jack callousness

jokes.text: what? But you're down there. Or am i?
jokes.setup: Jack asking Tracy to tackle Gavin from above
jokes.punchline: Tracy's confusion about which version of himself is where
jokes.explanation: Good payoff to the sex doll setup - Tracy's genuine confusion about his identity vs the doll

jokes.text: You know, a lot of people look down on sex dolls. But as we saw tonight,they save lives and bring families together.
jokes.setup: Reflecting on the evening's events
jokes.punchline: Treating sex dolls as wholesome family values product
jokes.explanation: Brilliant callback and reframing - turning a sex toy into a moral lesson about family values

jokes.text: How am i such an expert? I'm tracy jordan's sex doll.
jokes.setup: Tracy giving advice about sex dolls
jokes.punchline: Reveals he IS the sex doll giving the testimonial
jokes.explanation: Excellent meta twist - the testimonial comes from the product itself, perfect absurd ending
